name: Salt Lake / Aliamanu
desc: Military-adjacent neighborhoods near the airport — Salt Lake's shopping and Aliamanu's crater community combined, practical living.
insider: Salt Lake Shopping Center has some of the best cheap eats in Central Honolulu — Pho 97 and Mitsu-Ken garlic chicken are standouts
Aliamanu Military Reservation sits inside an extinct volcanic crater — the PX, commissary, and military facilities save families serious money
Tripler Army Medical Center on the hill above is impossible to miss with its coral-pink exterior — built in 1948 and the largest military hospital in the Pacific
Proximity to the airport means convenient travel but also jet noise, especially early morning departures — upper Salt Lake is quieter
Ala Lilikoi Community Park has a great playground and weekend soccer leagues — the local kid scene is strong here
Salt Lake was built in the 1960s-70s over former fishponds and wetlands — some areas have settling issues, so check foundations when buying
The neighborhood is one of the most ethnically diverse in Hawaii — you'll hear Tagalog, Samoan, Japanese, and English on any given block
Stadium Mall and the nearby Sam's Club are the main shopping hubs — nothing fancy but very practical
Red Hill fuel tank controversy put Aliamanu on the national news — the Navy's underground fuel storage contaminated the water supply, so verify your water source
Getting to Waikiki takes about 15 minutes with no traffic, but rush hour on the H-1 can easily triple that
